div.p: Requests arrive asynchronously, batch forms at timeout

Request 1: Total = 50ms wait + 40ms inference = 90ms Request 2: Total = 40ms wait + 40ms inference = 80ms Request 3: Total = 20ms wait + 40ms inference = 60ms Request 4: Total = 5ms wait + 40ms inference = 45ms Throughput gain: 4 requests / 90ms = 44 req/s vs sequential: 4 × 40ms = 160ms = 25 req/s → 1.76× improvement
The 50ms timeout bounds worst-case latency. First request waits longest but still gets response within acceptable SLA window. GPU processes all 4 in single batch, amortizing kernel launch overhead.
